channel.title: NBC Sports Fantasy Football News
channel.description: Latest player news and analysis for fantasy football from NBC Sports.

item.title: Browns tendered exclusive rights free agent WR Malachi Corley.
item.description: Cleveland scooped Corley off waivers after the Jets cut him at the end of training camp last year. A true gadget player, Corley earned more carries than catches, rushing 13 times compared to 11 grabs. He totaled 206 yards from scrimmage and returned kicks for Cleveland. The Browns also tendered WR Jamari Thrash and TE Brenden Bates.

item.title: Browns tendered exclusive rights free agent K Andre Szmyt.
item.description: Cleveland also tendered LB Winston Reid and LS Rex Sunahara. Szmyt went 24-of-27 on his field goal attempts in 2026. He missed one of his 26 PATs.

item.title: Lions tendered exclusive rights free agent K Jake Bates.
item.description: Bates’ numbers took a hit in 2025 compared to his previous year. He connected on just 79 percent of his attempts compared to 90 percent in 2024. Five of his seven misses came from at least 50 yards out and he was perfect within 40 yards. Even after the down season, Bates is still an above-average kicker and the ERFA tender costs Detroit next to nothing. Bates will remain a strong fantasy option in 2026.

item.title: Browns OC Travis Switzer said Deshaun Watson has to be “part of the equation” at quarterback.
item.description: The Browns are still laboring through the repercussions of the Watson trade. He will remain on the roster this year and was nearly ready to return from his torn Achilles at the end of the 2025 season. He should be a full-go for the start of the offseason program, putting him in line to compete with Dillon Gabriel and Shedeur Sanders for the starting job. The Browns could make another addition via free agency or the draft, but our hopes are not high for this room in 2026.

item.title: ESPN’s Adam Schefter reports the Seahawks are “not close” to reaching a contract extension with WR Rashid Shaheed.
item.description: Shaheed is expected to test the free agent market at the start of the new league year. The 27-year-old was acquired by the Super Bowl champions in the middle of the season, totaling a 15/188 line in nine regular season games and 3/78 in the playoffs with a kick return touchdown. Shaheed will receive interest as a deep ball receiver and return specialist; while a return to Seattle is still possible, the fact that the two sides were not close on an extension indicates Shaheed will have a new home for 2026.

item.title: ESPN’s Jeremy Fowler reports the Vikings are expected to release DT Jonathan Allen.
item.description: The Vikings have had to make serious cuts due to salary cap constraints heading into the offseason. Allen joins the line of players headed out the door, saving Minnesota $6.5 million in cap space. The 31-year-old signed a three-year deal with the team last March and tallied 3.5 sacks and 11 quarterback hits in his sole season as a Viking. Allen will have a sizeable market as a veteran interior pass rusher.

item.title: Commanders signed DT Shy Tuttle to a one-year contract extension.
item.description: Tuttle was set to hit free agency, but will instead return to Washington next season. He appeared in 10 games for the Titans and two games for the Commanders last season, mostly within a defensive line rotation. The veteran will provide the Commanders with depth again this upcoming season.

item.title: ESPN’s Adam Schefter reports the Buccaneers exercised the option on P Riley Dixon’s contract.
item.description: By exercising Dixon’s option, the Bucs prevent him from hitting free agency and bring him back for 2026. He can earn up to $3 million this upcoming season. Dixon averaged 44.7 yards per punt in 2025 and had a 6.3-percent touchback rate. The 32-year-old will kick for the Bucs again as he enters his eleventh season in the NFL.

item.title: Commanders signed DE Deatrich Wise to a one-year contract extension.
item.description: The Commanders are ensuring their defensive line depth is securing, extending DT Shy Tuttle and Wise to one-year deals. Wise saw his 2025 season with Washington end early with a quad injury in Week 2. The 31-year-old previously spent eight seasons with the Patriots, tallying 34 sacks in 126 games over that span. Wise will add depth to the edges of Washington’s defensive line.

item.title: ESPN’s Jeremy Fowler reports RB Sean Tucker will not receiver a restricted tender from the Buccaneers, making him an unrestricted free agent.
item.description: Tucker was set to be a restricted free agent, but the Buccaneers declined to give him a tender. The 24-year-old will now hit free agency after two seasons of showing flashes of promise in a crowded backfield. Tucker saw limited opportunities behind Rachaad White and Bucky Irving, but developed into a short-yardage back in 2025. Fantasy managers got big games out of him twice. Once with a 17 touches for 192 yards and two touchdowns against the Saints in 2024, and again with a 21 touches for 140 yards and three touchdowns against the Bills in 2025. Tucker will generate interest from teams looking to add a bruising back to their committee in 2026.
